steps.title: Use substitution since y is isolated
steps.content:
The first equation already has $y$ isolated as $y = 6x + 16$. Substitute this expression into the second equation $-7x - y = 36$:

$$-7x - (6x + 16) = 36$$

steps.title: Substitute back to find y
steps.content:
Use $x = -4$ in the first equation $y = 6x + 16$:

$$y = 6(-4) + 16$$
$$y = -24 + 16$$
$$y = -8$$

The solution is $(x, y) = (-4, -8)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
